directions.name: Restore accepted grouped_rows=8 hot-band consumer ordering
directions.hypothesis: The grouped_rows=16 branch was measured negative; returning to the accepted grouped_rows=8 window should recover the better PTX hot-band consumer behavior from round 56 and leave room for a narrower consumer-side tweak such as row-pair traversal order or another local ordering refinement.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Consumer-side PTX hot-band ordering and export latency in bf16_gemm_v1_tensor_core_fixed_hot_band_128x128_ptx_microkernel, especially around the grouped-row dispatch and the consumer/export handoff.
directions.risk: Moderate: a local ordering tweak can easily drift back into the grouped_rows=16 slow path or disturb the accepted PTX export-scratch behavior.
metrics_to_recheck: kernel runtime vs 24.713584 ms accepted base
directions.stop_condition: Stop if grouped_rows=8 restores the accepted runtime window and any local consumer-order tweak does not worsen dram or scoreboard metrics.

directions.name: Recover overlap behind the accepted wait_group_0 handoff
directions.hypothesis: After grouped_rows=8 is restored, the remaining gap may come from refill/consume overlap; a careful tweak around the accepted one-sync wait_group_0 handoff could reduce idle time without widening the stage pipeline.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Async refill overlap in the peeled PTX hot-band path, including the one-sync handoff in the PTX microkernel loop and the peeled hot-stage advance helper.
directions.risk: Medium: pushing overlap too far can revive the measured-negative grouped_rows=16 behavior or create a refill hazard.
directions.stop_condition: Stop only if overlap improves after grouped_rows=8 is restored and no extra syncs or hazards appear.

directions.name: Smaller locality retune as a closure path
directions.hypothesis: If the top two paths do not recover the loss, a smaller launch-order locality retune may still reduce hot-band stalls without introducing another broad structural change.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual launch-order locality and row-pair adjacency effects in the grouped hot-band consumer path.
directions.risk: High: broader grouped-row changes, warmup-order reopen, K32 cadence changes, extra-live B lookahead, unroll-1 base, CTA-level B repack, and broad shared-memory rewrites are explicitly rejected this round.
directions.stop_condition: Stop after one smaller locality trial; do not broaden into grouped_rows=16 or any rejected structural rewrite.

notes: Anchored to measured run 20260420_084554_bf16_gemm_v1_761d868 at 25.473536 ms. Round 56 accepted base at 24.713584 ms remains the target comparison; grouped_rows=16, warmup-order reopen, K32 cadence, extra-live B lookahead, unroll-1 base, CTA-level B repack, and broad shared-memory rewrites are rejected this round.
